text:
whoami should probably not exist and instead should be shown on `tenex-edge who` when run by an agent (i.e. we show the agent who they are and in what channel they are on)
 [...] What changed:
- `who` now includes the current agent identity when run inside an agent session.
- `whoami` CLI/RPC is removed.
- Local mentions resolve through agent-instance labels like `@haiku` / `@haiku1`, and p-tag the selected instance pubkey.
- Session codenames are gone from code paths: no generation, no session-start field, no resume-by-codename, no display/routing use.
- Product docs/wiki no longer carry active `codename` / `whoami` vocabulary, except historical transcript/citation evidence.
